title: Error Classes, Discriminated Results, Causes, Retry, and Session Recovery · Type-System Trace: Pokedex Ts Retry Policy
summary: Catch blocks treat unknown as Error without narrowing, HTTP and validation failures share prose, retry wraps non-idempotent state transitions, causes are lost, and any rejection closes the session. Predict the user decision, runtime value, strict type claim, promise state, API boundary, failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pokedex ts retry policy increment in causal typed recovery decision model. Learners progress through pre…
objectives: Retry only transient idempotent fetches under attempt, total time, spacing, fair-use, and AbortSignal limits.
Retain safe causal chains and operation identity while separating internal diagnostics from user-facing recovery text.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Pokedex Ts Retry Policy
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory TypeScript team handles case 3887 during a shared classroom network exercise. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to normalize unknown throws, classify causal results, bound retry, and preserve session recovery; the fixed PokéAPI origin and fair-use budget must remain enforced; then change one catch, owned record, export, location relation, or history transition without mutating prior state. Browser code uses pure bounded runtime models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, Node readline, host streams, signals, files, package installs, handle and load claims, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
